context.approach: User-uploaded screenshots reviewed by the multimodal GTM synthesis layer against the same seven UX/UI audit axes.

methodology.description: Screenshots are uploaded directly by the user and preserved as report evidence.
A multimodal model reviews visible UI evidence against seven GTM UX/UI axes.
The highest-impact issues are translated into sales-facing recommendations and a static report.

profile.visual_review_goal: Evaluate uploaded UI screenshots against the same seven GTM UX/UI audit axes used by the website audit mode.

result.site_summary: Two-page review of WeeFizz legal and contact pages showing consistent branding but with trust and microcopy issues in B2B context

task_execution.observation: Contact page clearly presents three contact methods but lacks actionable next steps beyond basic information; legal page is purely informational with no user tasks

flow_architecture.observation: Navigation is consistent across pages with logical footer placement for legal page, but contact page lacks contextual breadcrumbs for user journey

trust_accessibility.observation: Legal page provides comprehensive company details (RCS, SIRET, address) which builds credibility, but contact page misses social proof elements expected in B2B

ui_consistency.observation: Header/footer consistent across pages with uniform green color scheme and spacing; contact page uses consistent iconography for contact methods

visual_brand.observation: Green color scheme aligns with brand but contact page background image is generic office scene that doesn't reinforce B2B value proposition

content_microcopy.observation: Contact page labels are clear but booking link text 'Calendar google' is non-standard and may confuse international users; legal page uses precise regulatory language

market_alignment.observation: Contact page fails to leverage B2B trust signals (client logos, case studies); legal page overemphasizes compliance over commercial value

priority_issues.title: Non-standard booking link terminology
priority_issues.reason: Confusing terminology reduces conversion potential in international B2B context
priority_issues.evidence: Booking link under 'Prendre rendez-vous' reads 'Calendar google' instead of standard 'Google Calendar'
priority_issues.recommendation: Use standardized terminology 'Google Calendar' with proper capitalization

visual_region.description: Link text 'Calendar google' in booking section

visual_trust_findings.title: Generic background imagery on contact page
visual_trust_findings.evidence: Blurred office background with green overlay appears as generic stock imagery lacking authentic workplace context
visual_trust_findings.why_it_matters: In B2B, generic stock imagery undermines credibility and fails to demonstrate real-world client environment
visual_trust_findings.recommendation: Replace with custom imagery showing actual team/workspace or client-specific scenarios

strengths: Comprehensive legal information with verified company details (RCS/SIRET)
Consistent navigation and color scheme across pages
Clear contact information with multiple verified channels
result.market_positioning: Positioned as a compliant B2B service provider but lacks persuasive trust elements and value-driven storytelling needed for enterprise sales cycles

aiDiscoveredFindings.title: Generic background imagery on contact page
aiDiscoveredFindings.evidence: Blurred office background with green overlay appears as generic stock imagery lacking authentic workplace context
aiDiscoveredFindings.explanation: On Screenshot 2, the review found that 'Generic background imagery on contact page' may not be fully supported. Blurred office background with green overlay appears as generic stock imagery lacking authentic workplace context
aiDiscoveredFindings.whyItMatters: In B2B, generic stock imagery undermines credibility and fails to demonstrate real-world client environment
aiDiscoveredFindings.recommendation: Replace with custom imagery showing actual team/workspace or client-specific scenarios

aiDiscoveredFindings.title: Non-standard booking link terminology
aiDiscoveredFindings.evidence: Booking link under 'Prendre rendez-vous' reads 'Calendar google' instead of standard 'Google Calendar'
aiDiscoveredFindings.explanation: On Screenshot 2, the review found that 'Non-standard booking link terminology' may not be fully supported. Confusing terminology reduces conversion potential in international B2B context
aiDiscoveredFindings.whyItMatters: This matters because the value proposition and interaction cues are harder to understand than they should be. In a GTM context, visible friction in Screenshot 2 can reduce clarity, trust, or conversion readiness.
aiDiscoveredFindings.recommendation: Use standardized terminology 'Google Calendar' with proper capitalization

axes.name: Task Execution
axes.description: How clearly the product helps users complete key actions without friction.
axes.summary: Contact page clearly presents three contact methods but lacks actionable next steps beyond basic information; legal page is purely informational with no user tasks
axes.businessImpact: Friction in key tasks increases drop-off and weakens the product story in a live sales context.

strengths.title: Comprehensive legal information with verified company details (RCS/SIRET)
strengths.evidence: Comprehensive legal information with verified company details (RCS/SIRET)
strengths.explanation: Comprehensive legal information with verified company details (RCS/SIRET)
strengths.whyItMatters: This helps because core tasks demand more effort than they should.
strengths.recommendation: Preserve this strength while addressing higher-priority friction.

opportunities: Use the screenshot evidence to refine task execution before launch.
axes.visionObservation: Contact page clearly presents three contact methods but lacks actionable next steps beyond basic information; legal page is purely informational with no user tasks

axes.name: Flow & Architecture
axes.description: How understandable the information architecture and navigation model feel.
axes.summary: Navigation is consistent across pages with logical footer placement for legal page, but contact page lacks contextual breadcrumbs for user journey
axes.businessImpact: Weak architecture slows comprehension and makes the offer feel less mature.

axes.name: UI Consistency

axes.businessImpact: Inconsistent UI signals reduce perceived product maturity and scalability.

axes.name: Visual Brand
axes.description: How the visual direction expresses the brand and supports persuasive storytelling.
axes.summary: Green color scheme aligns with brand but contact page background image is generic office scene that doesn't reinforce B2B value proposition
axes.businessImpact: A weak visual narrative lowers memorability and product differentiation.

painPoints.title: Generic background imagery on contact page

painPoints.whyItMatters: In B2B, generic stock imagery undermines credibility and fails to demonstrate real-world client environment
painPoints.recommendation: Replace with custom imagery showing actual team/workspace or client-specific scenarios

axes.name: Content & Microcopy
axes.description: How clearly messaging, labels, and calls to action communicate value and next steps.
axes.summary: Contact page labels are clear but booking link text 'Calendar google' is non-standard and may confuse international users; legal page uses precise regulatory language
axes.businessImpact: Unclear messaging makes the value proposition harder to understand and repeat.

painPoints.title: Non-standard booking link terminology
painPoints.evidence: Booking link under 'Prendre rendez-vous' reads 'Calendar google' instead of standard 'Google Calendar'
painPoints.explanation: On Screenshot 2, the review found that 'Non-standard booking link terminology' may not be fully supported. Confusing terminology reduces conversion potential in international B2B context
painPoints.whyItMatters: This matters because the value proposition and interaction cues are harder to understand than they should be. In a GTM context, visible friction in Screenshot 2 can reduce clarity, trust, or conversion readiness.
painPoints.recommendation: Use standardized terminology 'Google Calendar' with proper capitalization

axes.name: Market Alignment
axes.description: How well the offer, audience cues, proof points, and CTA strategy support a GTM story.
axes.summary: Contact page fails to leverage B2B trust signals (client logos, case studies); legal page overemphasizes compliance over commercial value
axes.businessImpact: Weak GTM alignment makes it harder for prospects to see why this product fits them now.
